response.justification: Bogdan is a capable and well-rounded engineer with strong software craftsmanship instincts and broad experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, with DLang as a secondary focus, and while Java appears in his skills list, it is never a featured technology in any role. There is no mention of Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM-deep work. His one adjacent data point — writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI, a C IDE — is interesting but is neither JVM-based nor IDE-internals work in the sense this role demands.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistent practice of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing across multiple roles
Experience with high-throughput, performance-sensitive systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby) demonstrates ability to work on complex codebases
One tangential IDE tooling data point: wrote C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI, a real engineering IDE, showing some affinity for developer tooling
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth — Java appears only in the skills list and briefly at Altom; Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume
Zero exposure to Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise JVM framework, which are core technical focus areas for this role
No JVM internals experience (classloading, bytecode, GC tuning, build tooling like Gradle/Maven) — the role requires deep JVM understanding, not surface-level Java familiarity

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Strong craftsmanship and complex systems background
